project_name: <Name of your game or narrative project>
narrative_world: <A brief summary of the world. e.g. A cyberpunk city run by corporations, or a cozy magical bakery.>
geography: <The physical or fictional location. e.g. Neo-Tokyo, Japan or The Whispering Woods>
time_period: <When the story takes place. e.g. 2142, Present day, Victorian Era>
primary_setting: <The specific location where the game happens. e.g. A neon-lit ramen stand>
tone: <The mood of the game. e.g. Gritty, noir, cozy, slice-of-life>
genre: <The genre of the game. e.g. Narrative RPG, Visual Novel>

protagonist.name: <Name of the main character the player controls or follows>
protagonist.role: <What the protagonist does. e.g. Bounty hunter, baker, student>
protagonist.backstory: <A short paragraph about where the protagonist comes from and their past.>
protagonist.wants: <What the protagonist is actively trying to achieve in the story.>
protagonist.avoiding: <What the protagonist is running away from or fears happening.>
protagonist.why_still_here: <Why the protagonist remains in the primary setting despite their struggles.>

research.priority: <Topics for the AI to search. e.g. Cyberpunk tropes, baking techniques, local folklore>
research.avoid: <Things the AI should NOT include in its research or generation.>

character_rules: <Rule 1: e.g. Characters should feel grounded and realistic>
<Rule 2: e.g. Avoid giving characters magical powers unless stated>
<Rule 3: e.g. Dialogue should be terse and untrusting>

narrative_lens.description: <How the protagonist views the world. e.g. A paranoid detective looking for lies, or a hopeful artist looking for beauty.>
narrative_lens.instruction: <Instructions for the AI to write a FIRST IMPRESSION block based on the protagonist's unique perspective.>
